channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: The London Welsh Centre, 20th May
item.description:
Dear Sir/Madam, I am writing regarding the recently installed bench outside our building. We were extremely surprised to find that this had been placed there without any prior consultation or communication with us as the occupants/business directly affected. The installation of the cycle lane has already had a significant impact on our business, particularly in relation to deliveries, access, and the safe movement of people outside the building. The addition of a bench directly outside the premises has now created further concerns regarding access and safety. Unfortunately, the current placement is not suitable. It obstructs the space outside the building and we are concerned it will create ongoing access and safety issues for both staff and visitors. In addition, the positioning on the edge of the pavement appears inappropriate and potentially unsafe. Had we been consulted beforehand, we would have gladly discussed alternative locations nearby that may have worked better for both the council and ourselves. However, the current location does not work for our building or day-to-day operations. We would therefore kindly request that the bench is removed and relocated elsewhere as soon as possible. Please could you let us know when this will be reviewed and when arrangements will be made for its removal. Many thanks, Nicola
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Garden waste is being tipped onto the grass verge on the side of the road outside a property, 20th May
item.description:
Home owner in middle of Little Birches does not have (pay for) a 'Brown' garden collection bin like other residents, so they simply dump waste on the roadside verge in front of the property. This is an unsightly mess which forces the council to clear it up and it cheats the need to pay for a bin. Why is this homeowner allowed to simply tip their garden waste onto the side of the road / verge without any penalty?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 29, Little Birches, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 8x4 sheet of wood hanging over main road, 20th May
item.description:
This is extremely dangerous as could injure someone/something

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Wolvercote Road, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Side of 118 Godstow Road: huge cardboard box; metal clotheshorse and rubbish scattered around., 20th May
item.description:
All this fly-tipping is under tree on grass verge at side of 118 Godstow Road.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Godstow Road, Abbey Wood

item.title: Next to 71 Boxgrove Road: mattress., 20th May
item.description:
Mattress is propped up against brick wall next to 71 Boxgrove Road. Access is vis Throwley Close.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Throwley Close, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Old pallet and redchair, 20th May
item.description:
Pallet left on pavement by 210 church manor way, falls over and blocks pavement, so my aged dad cannot get past on mobility scooter. Also old red chair same issue.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Church Manorway, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox cub in grass verge on pavement between Glenlea Rd and Westmount Rd, 20th May
item.description:
Previously reported but appears to have been referred to TFL. It on pavement area so not sure if this correct.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Westmount Road, Well Hall, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Dead animal in the carriageway or footway

item.title: Rubbish strewn along pavements and gutters, 20th May
item.description:
Rubbish strewn along pavements and gutters, some has been here a while., some from recycling collection today also dead rat on pavement. I reported this last week and nothing has been done although I have received a closed incident notice from t yourself. Please sweep this road it is disgusting.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Palace Grove, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog poo on pavement and road, 20th May
item.description:
Dog poo on road and pavement been here for 2 days flys buzzing and people stepping. On it, outside 33 Listria park bad smell

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Listria Park, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Looks like damaged sewer works, 20th May
item.description:
Road permanently wet with possible sewage

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 33, Great Elms Road, Widmore, Greater London

item.title: Corrode metal footbridge, 20th May
item.description:
The footbridge at the end of Pembroke Road that connects to West Street has corroded away. This needs to be made safe. It goes over a railway line & a dual carriageway way

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pembroke Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Salt, 20th May
item.description:
In oaklands road carpark Bexleyheath there is a pile of left over gritting salt in the far left corner of carpark making it look really messy.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Albion Road, Bexley, Bexleyheath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trunks dumped, 20th May
item.description:
Contractor dealing with complaint about tree stumps dumped ref -9469182 shredded most but then instead of removing large trunks from the park just dumped them in the copse of woods behind Finucane Drive

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Finucane Drive, Ramsden, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped hessian sheet, 20th May
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Queensway, Poverest, Greater London

item.title: Two tyres abandoned at end of road, 20th May
item.description:
The tyres have moved about, been kicked around etc but there are two to remove. Also other detritus to remove at this end of the road, shopping basket, part of a bike, aerosol cans.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Dunbar Avenue, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
